# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. bd dolt pull            (pull beads updates from main)
[ ] 4. git commit -m "..."     (commit code changes)
```

**Note:** This is an ephemeral branch (no upstream). Code is merged to main locally, not pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: run `bd dolt pull` at session start
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads updates from Dolt remote
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
bd dolt pull                # Pull latest beads from main
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit your changes
# Merge to main when ready (local merge, not push)
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

## Persistent Memories (4)

Stored via `bd remember`. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`. Remove with `bd forget <key>`.

### issue-102-fully-resolved-with-4-distinct-fixes
Issue #102 fully resolved with 4 distinct fixes (see commits on worktree-102 branch): Bug A=disable stale-tool timer for Claude Code in core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/policy.go; Bug B=new LastWasUserInterrupt field in domain metrics, set only on the '[Request interrupted by user' text marker, used by state_classifier cancellation rule instead of LastToolResultWasError; Bug C=removed toolUseCount/toolResultCount counters in pkg/tailer/tailer.go, HasOpenToolCall now derived from len(lastOpenToolNames) — single source of truth eliminates counter desync on orphan tool_result events from --continue/compact; Bug D=allow-list of terminal stop_reasons {end_turn,stop_sequence,refusal,tool_use} in claudecode/parser.go, everything else (including max_tokens, pause_turn) becomes assistant_streaming. Total flicker reduction across 4 fixtures: 7368 → 1 (the 1 residual is a legitimate 5.8s turn_done-triggered ready episode, not a bug). Replay harness lives at core/cmd/replay-session with scripts in scripts/replay-fixtures.sh and scripts/find-flicker-sessions.sh, fixtures under testdata/issue-102/fixtures.

### issue-102-has-two-distinct-flicker-root-causes
Issue #102 has TWO distinct flicker root causes (replay of current Claude Code session captures both): (1) 15s defaultStaleToolTimeout fires during long Bash runs (working→waiting→working), as previously documented. (2) ClassifyState misinterprets benign tool failures as ESC cancellation: in core/application/services/state_classifier.go the rule '(state==working|waiting) && !HasOpenToolCall && LastEventType==user && LastToolResultWasError → ready (cancellation)' fires whenever any tool returns is_error=true (grep with no matches, failed Bash, etc.), bouncing the session to ready then immediately back to working on the next event. The fix needs to distinguish actual ESC interrupts (user message text starts with '[Request interrupted by user') from normal tool failures. The current claudecode/parser.go at line 84 already detects the ESC text marker — the cancellation rule should check that signal, not just LastToolResultWasError.

### issue-102-root-cause-is-the-15s-defaultstaletooltimeout
Issue #102 root cause is the 15s defaultStaleToolTimeout in core/application/services/session_detector.go, NOT the MessageHistory sliding window. The replay tool (core/cmd/replay-session) shows the pattern clearly: any Bash tool that runs >15s repeatedly trips the stale-tool timer → waiting → tool result arrives → working. On a 558h fixture it produced 82 flickers / 42 stale-timer fires, all on Bash. Fix should bump the timeout for Bash specifically or use a more conservative anchor-event approach. Replay reproductions live in testdata/issue-102/reports/.

### pr-106-bundles-two-flicker-fixes-1-97
PR #106 bundles two flicker fixes: (1) #97 stale-tool timer + StatePolicy abstraction removed (19 files +46/-806); (2) Layer 1 from #102 — Claude Code parser splits [Request interrupted by user] (ESC, sets IsUserInterrupt) from [Request interrupted by user for tool use] (denial, sets new IsToolDenial). Cancellation rule only fires on ESC now, suppressing working→ready→working flicker on every denial. LastWasToolDenial propagates through tailer + domain.SessionMetrics. Layer 2 tracked as GH #108 — uses PermissionRequest hook (NOT Notification), reuses irrlichd HTTP server for ingestion via POST /api/v1/hooks/claudecode, claudecode-adapter-isolated, exposes single generic PermissionPending boolean on shared metrics (avoids reintroducing the deleted StatePolicy abstraction). Key correction: bypassPermissions is NOT a sufficient gate predictor — protected paths (.git/.claude/.vscode/.idea/.husky) still prompt.

title:	Claude sessions can flap in and out of the dashboard when multiple live sessions share one cwd
state:	OPEN
author:	ingo-eichhorst
labels:	bug
comments:	0
assignees:	
projects:	
milestone:	
number:	109
--
## Summary

A Claude session can appear in the session status display, disappear a few seconds later, then reappear again. This is not a frontend-only problem. `irrlichd` is deleting and recreating the session in the backend.

Observed example:
- Session: `93a9c951-b0f3-4af4-9a26-675ce3b6eb58`
- Competing session: `b91b4b0a-58e2-4481-954a-5b57ca3305e8`
- Repo cwd: `/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht`

## What happened

From the local Irrlicht event log on April 8, 2026:
- `23:17:43`: `b91b4b0a-...` was assigned PID `91149`
- Immediately after: `93a9c951-... replaced by new session b91b4b0a-... (same pid 91149) — deleting`
- Later the reverse happened repeatedly:
  - `23:20:34`
  - `23:22:04`
  - `23:23:41`
  - `23:24:39`
- Both transcript files were still active while this was happening.

There was also an earlier cleanup path where a freshly recreated session sat in `ready` with `pid=0` long enough to hit the fast cleanup rule:
- `ready session with no PID for >30s, deleting`

## Root cause

The Claude adapter currently discovers session ownership by matching `claude` processes on `cwd` only:
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid.go`
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/discovery.go`

That is too weak when multiple live Claude processes share the same repository directory.

In the observed case there were at least two live `claude` processes with the same cwd:
- `91149` -> Claude metadata mapped this PID to session `93a9c951-b0f3-4af4-9a26-675ce3b6eb58`
- `78223` -> Claude metadata mapped this PID to session `d42c9f5d-4f29-4460-afc0-5c469b49d45c`

At the same time, transcript `b91b4b0a-58e2-4481-954a-5b57ca3305e8.jsonl` was still receiving writes.

Because PID discovery only knows `cwd`, Irrlicht can assign the wrong PID to a transcript when several `claude` processes share that directory. Once a session gets assigned a PID that is already in use, `PIDManager.HandlePIDAssigned` deletes the other non-child session with the same PID as stale.

So the actual buggy chain is:
1. File watcher sees activity for transcript `b91b...`
2. Claude PID discovery matches by shared cwd only
3. `b91b...` gets bound to PID `91149`, even though that PID is already the known owner of `93a9...`
4. Duplicate-PID cleanup deletes `93a9...`
5. Fresh transcript activity for `93a9...` triggers `--continue` re-creation
6. The cycle repeats in the other direction

## Why the current logic is unsafe

The code currently assumes that for non-child Claude sessions:
- one PID maps to one session
- if a new session claims an already-used PID, the old session is stale

That assumption only holds if PID discovery is reliable. With `cwd`-only matching, it is not reliable when multiple Claude sessions are open in the same repo.

## Potential fix

Tighten Claude PID ownership before running duplicate-PID deletion.

Preferred direction:
- Replace the Claude `cwd`-only PID heuristic with a stronger source of truth from Claude's own session metadata under `~/.claude/sessions/*.json`.
- Match by `sessionId -> pid` first.
- Use `cwd` only as a fallback when there is no direct Claude metadata entry.

Additional guardrails:
- In duplicate-PID cleanup, do not immediately delete another session just because a new session resolved to the same PID via a weak heuristic.
- If PID ownership came from fallback matching rather than a direct session-to-pid mapping, treat the collision as ambiguous and keep both sessions until ownership is resolved.
- Consider disabling the 30s `ready && pid==0` fast-delete for newly recreated Claude sessions while PID discovery is still ambiguous.

## Expected outcome after fix

If multiple Claude processes share the same cwd:
- each transcript should bind to its actual Claude-owned PID when available
- ambiguous ownership should not trigger destructive duplicate-session deletion
- the dashboard should stop showing sessions that flap in and out due to backend delete/recreate cycles

## Relevant code

- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid.go`
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/discovery.go`
- `core/application/services/pid_manager.go`
- `core/application/services/session_detector.go`

There was also an earlier cleanup path where a freshly recreated session sat in `ready` with `pid=0` long enough to hit the fast cleanup rule:
- `ready session with no PID for >30s, deleting`

## Root cause

The Claude adapter currently discovers session ownership by matching `claude` processes on `cwd` only:
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid.go`
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/discovery.go`

That is too weak when multiple live Claude processes share the same repository directory.

In the observed case there were at least two live `claude` processes with the same cwd:
- `91149` -> Claude metadata mapped this PID to session `93a9c951-b0f3-4af4-9a26-675ce3b6eb58`
- `78223` -> Claude metadata mapped this PID to session `d42c9f5d-4f29-4460-afc0-5c469b49d45c`

At the same time, transcript `b91b4b0a-58e2-4481-954a-5b57ca3305e8.jsonl` was still receiving writes.

Because PID discovery only knows `cwd`, Irrlicht can assign the wrong PID to a transcript when several `claude` processes share that directory. Once a session gets assigned a PID that is already in use, `PIDManager.HandlePIDAssigned` deletes the other non-child session with the same PID as stale.

So the actual buggy chain is:
1. File watcher sees activity for transcript `b91b...`
2. Claude PID discovery matches by shared cwd only
3. `b91b...` gets bound to PID `91149`, even though that PID is already the known owner of `93a9...`
4. Duplicate-PID cleanup deletes `93a9...`
5. Fresh transcript activity for `93a9...` triggers `--continue` re-creation
6. The cycle repeats in the other direction

## Why the current logic is unsafe

The code currently assumes that for non-child Claude sessions:
- one PID maps to one session
- if a new session claims an already-used PID, the old session is stale

That assumption only holds if PID discovery is reliable. With `cwd`-only matching, it is not reliable when multiple Claude sessions are open in the same repo.

## Potential fix

Tighten Claude PID ownership before running duplicate-PID deletion.

Preferred direction:
- Replace the Claude `cwd`-only PID heuristic with a stronger source of truth from Claude's own session metadata under `~/.claude/sessions/*.json`.
- Match by `sessionId -> pid` first.
- Use `cwd` only as a fallback when there is no direct Claude metadata entry.

Additional guardrails:
- In duplicate-PID cleanup, do not immediately delete another session just because a new session resolved to the same PID via a weak heuristic.
- If PID ownership came from fallback matching rather than a direct session-to-pid mapping, treat the collision as ambiguous and keep both sessions until ownership is resolved.
- Consider disabling the 30s `ready && pid==0` fast-delete for newly recreated Claude sessions while PID discovery is still ambiguous.

## Expected outcome after fix

If multiple Claude processes share the same cwd:
- each transcript should bind to its actual Claude-owned PID when available
- ambiguous ownership should not trigger destructive duplicate-session deletion
- the dashboard should stop showing sessions that flap in and out due to backend delete/recreate cycles

## Relevant code

- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid.go`
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/discovery.go`
- `core/application/services/pid_manager.go`
- `core/application/services/session_detector.go`

Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary report.

## Comprehensive PID Discovery & Duplicate Cleanup Architecture Report

### 1. Claude PID Discovery (CWD-based matching)

**File:** `/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid.go` (lines 1-12)

Claude Code uses CWD-only matching via a thin wrapper:
- **`DiscoverPID(cwd, transcriptPath string, disambiguate func([]int) int)`** — delegates to `processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWD("claude", cwd, disambiguate)`
- Irrlicht does **NOT** read Claude's session metadata files (`~/.claude/sessions/*.json`). Session metadata is entirely derived from transcript file activity.

### 2. Process Lifecycle Discovery (CWD Matching Implementation)

**File:** `/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/discovery.go` (lines 16-58)

**`DiscoverPIDByCWD(processName, cwd string, disambiguate func([]int) int) int`** — the root cause of the flapping:
- Uses `pgrep -x "claude"` to find all `claude` processes (line 20)
- For each PID, calls `ProcessCWD(pid)` via `lsof -p <pid> -d cwd` to get its working directory (osutil.go:71-84)
- Matches PIDs where `dir == cwd` (exact string match, line 35)
- When multiple PIDs match, delegates to `disambiguate(pids)` callback to select one
- **Critical flaw:** Multiple Claude processes in same cwd all match identically; disambiguation is necessary but insufficient

**Helper:** `ProcessCWD(pid int)` (osutil.go:71-84) — uses `lsof -p <pid> -d cwd -Fn` to extract CWD

### 3. Process Lifecycle Discovery — Other Agents

Alternative discovery strategies exposed in `processlifecycle/discovery.go`:
- **`DiscoverPIDByTranscriptWriter(transcriptPath)`** (lines 111-149) — used by Codex/Pi; finds process with transcript file open for write via `lsof <path>`. Reliable because transcript file is opened exactly once per session.
- **`DiscoverPIDByCmdPattern(pattern, cwd string, ...)`** (lines 64-105) — used by Pi; matches command-line pattern + CWD. Also suffers from CWD-only disambiguation.

### 4. PID Assignment & Duplicate Cleanup

**File:** `/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core/application/services/pid_manager.go`

**`HandlePIDAssigned(pid int, sessionID string)`** (lines 129-190) — **THIS IS WHERE FLAPPING OCCURS**:

1. **Stores pending PID** (lines 136-138) — saved to `pendingPIDs` map for deferred application in `processActivity`
2. **Direct save to repo** (lines 141-148) — immediately persists PID to disk
3. **Registers with ProcessWatcher** (lines 151-156) — starts exit monitoring via kqueue
4. **Duplicate cleanup** (lines 159-189) — the problematic logic:
   - Skips cleanup if session is a subagent (`ParentSessionID != ""`)
   - **Iterates all sessions** and deletes non-subagent sessions with matching PID (lines 172-189)
   - Condition: `old.SessionID != sessionID && old.PID == pid && old.ParentSessionID == "" && !strings.HasPrefix(old.SessionID, "proc-")`
   - **Root cause:** When two Claude processes run in same cwd, each newly-discovered transcript binds to the same PID; the older session gets deleted, triggering a re-discover cycle that alternately claims the PID.

**`TryDiscoverPID(sessionID, cwd, transcriptPath, adapter string) bool`** (lines 225-265):
- Calls adapter-specific discovery function (`pm.pidDiscovers[adapter]`)
- Uses smart `disambiguate` callback (lines 242-256) that prefers unclaimed PIDs but allows claimed ones (/clear scenario)
- Returns true if PID was found, false otherwise

**`DiscoverPIDWithRetry(...)`** (lines 270-284) — async discovery with backoff (500ms, 1s, 2s)

**`ConsumePendingPID(sessionID string)`** (lines 195-203) — atomic PID application during `processActivity`

### 5. Session Detector & Stale Session Cleanup

**File:** `/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core/application/services/session_detector.go`

**Key rule — "ready && pid==0 for >30s" cleanup** (lines 365-371 in `CheckPIDLiveness`):
```go
if state.PID == 0 && state.State == session.StateReady &&
    time.Since(time.Unix(state.UpdatedAt, 0)) > 30*time.Second {
  // delete session
}
```

**Other cleanup paths:**
- `seedFromDisk()` (lines 526-602) — on startup, re-evaluates stale session states, recomputes metrics
- `SweepDeadPIDs(ctx)` (lines 286-390 in pid_manager.go) — periodic liveness sweep every 5-15s, detects dead PIDs via `syscall.Kill(pid, 0)`, handles stale ready sessions and orphans

**Child session cleanup:**
- Child sessions (subagents) are deleted when parent transitions to ready (line 454-455)
- Child cleanup on seed (lines 355-360): children are deleted if ready OR transcript is stale

### 6. Session Metadata Fields

**File:** `/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core/domain/session/session.go` (lines 151-191)

**Key fields for disambiguation:**
- `SessionID` — transcript-derived session ID (immutable, unique to transcript)
- `PID` — process ID (can be shared across multiple sessions in same cwd)
- `ParentSessionID` — links child sessions to parent (subagent identifier)
- `TranscriptPath` — full path to transcript file (unique per session)
- `CWD` — working directory (shared when running multiple Claude processes in same repo)
- `FirstSeen` — timestamp when session was first detected
- `UpdatedAt` — last update timestamp
- `Adapter` — source agent ("claude-code", "codex", "pi", etc.)

### 7. Existing Tests

**File:** `/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core/application/services/session_detector_test.go`

**PID-related tests:**
- `TestSessionDetector_HandleProcessExit_DeletesSession` (line 557) — process exit deletes session
- `TestSessionDetector_HandlePIDAssigned_CleansUpOldSession` (line 1145) — verifies /clear cleanup logic
- Multiple tests around subagent PID handling (lines 990-1000, 1130-1135)
- Tests verify subagents sharing parent PID are NOT deleted

### 8. Surprising Architecture Details

1. **No Claude session metadata integration** — irrlicht never reads `~/.claude/sessions/*.json`. Session identity comes entirely from transcript file paths and PID discovery.

2. **CWD collision inherent** — multiple Claude processes in the same working directory will match identically in CWD discovery; the system relies on disambiguate callback + PID reassignment to separate them.

3. **Pending PID pattern** — `HandlePIDAssigned` saves twice (direct + pending) to handle races with concurrent `processActivity` state transitions.

4. **Subagent exemption** — subagents (child sessions) share parent's PID; cleanup logic explicitly skips them to prevent parent deletion when a child claims the same PID.

5. **Stale transcript detection** — `isStaleTranscript()` (helpers.go:14-23) checks file mtime; orphan transcripts >2 minutes old are skipped during new session creation.

### 9. The Fix Opportunity

The flapping cycle:
- Multiple Claude processes in same cwd → CWD-only matching finds all of them
- Each new transcript tries to bind to "the" PID (via disambiguate's highest-PID logic)
- `HandlePIDAssigned` deletes the older session with that PID
- Later, a re-discover for the now-orphaned transcript finds the other process
- Cycle repeats, causing dashboard flapping

**Fix strategy must** differentiate sessions running in the same cwd by something stronger than CWD, such as:
- SessionID from transcript (unique per session)
- Process lifetime correlation (e.g., earliest process child → oldest session)
- Transcript file identity as a tiebreaker

All changes should reuse existing abstractions: disambiguate callback, ProcessCWD, transcript path extraction.

toolUseResult.content:
# Plan: Fix Claude Session Flapping from PID Collisions (Issue #109)

## Context

When two or more live `claude` processes share the same repo cwd, Irrlicht sessions repeatedly disappear and reappear in the dashboard. `irrlichd` is deleting and recreating the session in the backend on a cycle.

Root cause (per the issue's diagnosis, confirmed via code read):

1. `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid.go:9` — `claudecode.DiscoverPID` delegates to `processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWD("claude", cwd, ...)`, which matches every `claude` process whose `lsof cwd` equals the session cwd. When several match, the `disambiguate` callback picks one (prefers unclaimed, falls back to highest PID).
2. `core/application/services/pid_manager.go:129` — `HandlePIDAssigned` then **deletes any other non-subagent session holding the same PID** as "stale" (this is the `/clear` cleanup path). With cwd-only matching, a newly detected transcript can claim a PID that already legitimately belongs to a different live session, destroying the rival session.
3. On the next write to the deleted session's transcript, `processActivity` re-creates it (`session_detector.go:361` — the `deletedCooldown`/`--continue` re-creation path), assigns it the *other* PID, and the cycle flips. The issue also mentions that occasionally the re-created session sits in `ready` with `pid=0` long enough to hit the 30-second fast-delete at `pid_manager.go:365`.

**Good news found during exploration:** Claude Code already writes a direct PID↔sessionId↔cwd mapping to disk. Each live process has a file at `~/.claude/sessions/<pid>.json`:

```json
{"pid":79982,"sessionId":"2f7a33aa-6881-44b3-990d-b87b75fe82c3","cwd":"/Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/.claude/worktrees/109","startedAt":1775767889862,"kind":"interactive","entrypoint":"cli"}
```

Irrlicht does not currently read this file. The transcript filename is already Claude's canonical sessionId (`fswatcher/watcher.go:332` `extractSessionID`), so we can look up PID by sessionId in O(n files) without touching the rest of the code.

## Goal

Replace the weak cwd-only heuristic with a strong metadata-driven lookup, and prevent the fallback path from ever triggering the destructive duplicate-PID cleanup. Ambiguous discovery should yield "no PID yet" and retry, not a confident-but-wrong answer.

## Approach

A two-layer fix in the Claude adapter only. No new abstractions, no signature changes to `PIDDiscoverFunc`, no changes to Codex/Pi discovery.

### Layer 1 — Authoritative metadata lookup (primary fix)

Rewrite `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid.go` so `DiscoverPID` does the following, in order:

1. **Derive Claude sessionId from `transcriptPath`.** Claude stores transcripts at `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<sessionId>.jsonl`, so `strings.TrimSuffix(filepath.Base(transcriptPath), ".jsonl")` is the canonical UUID. If `transcriptPath` is empty, skip to step 3 (fallback).
2. **Scan `~/.claude/sessions/*.json`.** For each file:
   - Read + `json.Unmarshal` into a small struct `{ PID int; SessionID string "json:\"sessionId\""; CWD string }`.
   - If `rec.SessionID == derivedSessionID` → verify `syscall.Kill(rec.PID, 0) == nil` (process alive). If alive, return `(rec.PID, nil)`. This is the **strong match**.
   - Also opportunistically build a set of `claimedMetaPIDs` — PIDs that some metadata file owns — to use in step 3 as a negative filter.
3. **Fallback — restricted cwd matching.** Call `processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWD("claude", cwd, disambiguate)` but wrap the callback so that:
   - PIDs present in `claimedMetaPIDs` but bound to a *different* sessionId are excluded (they belong to someone else, with certainty).
   - If after exclusion **more than one candidate remains**, return 0 (i.e. "unknown, retry"). Never allow the fallback to guess between competing live processes in the same cwd. `DiscoverPIDWithRetry` already retries at 500ms / 1s / 2s, which is plenty of time for Claude to finish writing its session metadata file on startup.
   - If exactly one candidate remains, return it.

Key implementation notes:

3. On the next write to the deleted session's transcript, `processActivity` re-creates it (`session_detector.go:361` — the `deletedCooldown`/`--continue` re-creation path), assigns it the *other* PID, and the cycle flips. ```json


Irrlicht does not currently read this file. The transcript filename is already Claude's canonical sessionId (`fswatcher/watcher.go:332` `extractSessionID`), so we can look up PID by sessionId in O(n files) without touching the rest of the code.

## Goal

Replace the weak cwd-only heuristic with a strong metadata-driven lookup, and prevent the fallback path from ever triggering the destructive duplicate-PID cleanup. Ambiguous discovery should yield "no PID yet" and retry, not a confident-but-wrong answer.

## Approach

A two-layer fix in the Claude adapter only. No new abstractions, no signature changes to `PIDDiscoverFunc`, no changes to Codex/Pi discovery.

### Layer 1 — Authoritative metadata lookup (primary fix)

Rewrite `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid.go` so `DiscoverPID` does the following, in order:

1. **Derive Claude sessionId from `transcriptPath`.** Claude stores transcripts at `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<sessionId>.jsonl`, so `strings.TrimSuffix(filepath.Base(transcriptPath), ".jsonl")` is the canonical UUID. If `transcriptPath` is empty, skip to step 3 (fallback).
2. **Scan `~/.claude/sessions/*.json`.** For each file:
   - Read + `json.Unmarshal` into a small struct `{ PID int; SessionID string "json:\"sessionId\""; CWD string }`.
   - If `rec.SessionID == derivedSessionID` → verify `syscall.Kill(rec.PID, 0) == nil` (process alive). If alive, return `(rec.PID, nil)`. This is the **strong match**.
   - Also opportunistically build a set of `claimedMetaPIDs` — PIDs that some metadata file owns — to use in step 3 as a negative filter.
3. **Fallback — restricted cwd matching.** Call `processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWD("claude", cwd, disambiguate)` but wrap the callback so that:
   - PIDs present in `claimedMetaPIDs` but bound to a *different* sessionId are excluded (they belong to someone else, with certainty).
   - If after exclusion **more than one candidate remains**, return 0 (i.e. "unknown, retry"). Never allow the fallback to guess between competing live processes in the same cwd. `DiscoverPIDWithRetry` already retries at 500ms / 1s / 2s, which is plenty of time for Claude to finish writing its session metadata file on startup.
   - If exactly one candidate remains, return it.

Key implementation notes:
- Expand `~` via `os.UserHomeDir()`; do **not** hardcode `/Users/...`. Cache the directory path at package init.
- Be defensive about garbage/stale files — ignore JSON parse errors and any record whose PID is dead; do not return them.
- Keep the function signature `DiscoverPID(cwd, transcriptPath string, disambiguate func([]int) int) (int, error)` unchanged. The disambiguate callback passed by `PIDManager.TryDiscoverPID` is still used in the fallback; the strong path doesn't need it.

The `readyTTL` path below it (the normal idle cleanup) is unchanged, so truly abandoned `ready/pid==0` zombies still get collected, just via the slower TTL path instead of the aggressive 30s path.

I considered adding a "confidence" flag to `PIDDiscoverFunc` so `HandlePIDAssigned` could skip duplicate cleanup on weak matches. Rejected because:
- It forces signature changes on Codex and Pi for no benefit (their transcript-writer discovery is already strong).
- Layer 1's fallback restriction already guarantees `HandlePIDAssigned` never sees a weak, ambiguous Claude match — the fallback now either returns a definitively unambiguous PID or 0.

2. **`core/application/services/pid_manager.go`** — add the `!isStaleTranscript(state.TranscriptPath)` guard on the 30s fast-delete branch around line 365-371.
3. **`core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid_test.go`** (new) — unit tests using a `t.TempDir()` fake `~/.claude/sessions/` directory (inject via a package-level `var sessionsDir = defaultSessionsDir()` so tests can override). Cases:
   - Strong match returns the metadata PID even if cwd has multiple live `claude` processes.
   - Stale metadata file (dead PID) is skipped.
   - Missing metadata file falls back to cwd matching.
   - Fallback with two live processes in the same cwd returns 0 (ambiguous), not a guess.
   - Fallback with exactly one live process returns it.
4. **`core/application/services/session_detector_test.go`** — extend or add a test that sets up two sessions with the same PID in the repo and verifies the fast-delete guard respects a fresh transcript mtime. After the fix: both sessions remain visible continuously, and the event log shows no `replaced by new session` deletions.
3. **Event log grep.** `tail -F ~/Library/Logs/irrlicht/events.log | grep -E 'replaced by new session|ready session with no PID'` during the repro. Both messages should be absent after the fix.

Reuse existing helpers (the file already has `TestSessionDetector_HandlePIDAssigned_CleansUpOldSession` at line 1145 as a template).

- `processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWD` at `core/adapters/inbound/agents/processlifecycle/discovery.go:16` — unchanged, called as the fallback.
- `processlifecycle.FindProcesses` (pgrep wrapper) and `ProcessCWD` (lsof wrapper) — used transitively by the fallback.

1. **Unit tests.** `go test ./core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/...` and `go test ./core/application/services/...` — must pass, including the new pid_test.go cases.
2. **Manual repro of the flap.** In two terminals, both `cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht && claude`. Let both sessions run for ~5 minutes with the daemon watching. content.text:
## Implementation complete

Fixed GH ingo-eichhorst/Irrlicht#109 per the approved plan. Tracking in beads as `irrlicht-cw3`.

**Layer 1 — authoritative PID discovery** (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid.go`)
- `DiscoverPID` now scans `~/.claude/sessions/<pid>.json`, which Claude writes for every live process and contains a direct `{pid, sessionId, cwd}` mapping. When the target sessionId (derived from the transcript filename) matches a live PID, it returns immediately — no cwd guessing.
- The cwd fallback is restricted: any PID already claimed by a different session in metadata is excluded, and if more than one live candidate remains, the fallback returns 0 (unknown — retry later) instead of stealing a rival's PID. `DiscoverPIDWithRetry` already retries at 500ms/1s/2s, which absorbs the metadata-write latency.
- Dead PIDs, corrupt JSON, and missing metadata are all handled defensively.

**Layer 2 — guard the 30s fast-delete** (`core/application/services/pid_manager.go:371-375`)
- `CheckPIDLiveness` now requires `isStaleTranscript(TranscriptPath)` before fast-deleting a `ready && pid==0` session. A fresh transcript with no PID means discovery is still catching up — don't delete. Abandoned zombies still get collected by the slower `readyTTL` path.

**Tests**
- `adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid_test.go` — 9 cases covering strong match, multi-file selection, dead PID, corrupt JSON, ambiguous fallback (returns 0), single-candidate fallback, metadata exclusion of competing PIDs, empty transcript path, and `sessionIDFromTranscript`.
- `application/services/pid_manager_internal_test.go` — in-package tests for the Layer 2 guard: fresh transcript NOT deleted, stale transcript still deleted.
- Full tree `go test ./adapters/... ./application/... ./domain/... ./ports/...` → all green. `go vet` clean.

**Not touched**
- `PIDDiscoverFunc` signature — unchanged, so Codex and Pi adapters are untouched.
- `HandlePIDAssigned` duplicate-cleanup logic — unchanged; the `/clear` path still works because `<pid>.json` overwrites in place when Claude rotates its sessionId.
- `processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWD` — unchanged, still the right default for agents without session metadata.

Changed files:
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid.go` (rewrite)
- `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid_test.go` (new)
- `core/application/services/pid_manager.go` (1-line guard + comment)
- `core/application/services/pid_manager_internal_test.go` (new)

Ready for the manual end-to-end verification in the plan (two `claude` sessions in the same repo + tail the event log for `replaced by new session` and `ready session with no PID`).

# Build & Restart macOS Dev Stack

Build the irrlicht daemon and Swift app, then replace all running instances with the new builds.

## Steps

1. **Build the Go daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && go build -o bin/irrlichd ./cmd/irrlichd
   ```

2. **Build the Swift app**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos && swift build 2>&1 | tail -5
   ```

3. **Kill all running irrlicht processes** (production app, production daemon, debug app, debug daemon)
   ```bash
   pkill -f "Irrlicht.app" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" 2>/dev/null
   pkill -f irrlichd 2>/dev/null
   sleep 2
   ```

4. **Clean up stale socket**
   ```bash
   rm -f /Users/ingo/.local/share/irrlicht/irrlichd.sock
   ```

5. **Start the dev daemon**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/core && nohup ./bin/irrlichd > /tmp/irrlichd-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

6. **Wait for daemon to be ready** — confirm port 7837 is listening before launching the app
   ```bash
   sleep 2 && lsof -iTCP:7837 -sTCP:LISTEN -P -n 2>/dev/null
   ```

7. **Start the dev Swift app**
   ```bash
   nohup /Users/ingo/projects/irrlicht/platforms/macos/.build/arm64-apple-macosx/debug/Irrlicht > /tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log 2>&1 & disown
   ```

8. **Verify** — confirm both processes are running and the daemon is serving sessions
   ```bash
   pgrep -f "bin/irrlichd" && pgrep -f "\.build.*Irrlicht" && curl -s http://localhost:7837/api/v1/sessions | head -1
   ```

## Notes
- The production Irrlicht.app (from DMG) bundles its own daemon. It MUST be killed before starting the dev daemon, otherwise port 7837 will be occupied.
- Daemon logs: `/tmp/irrlichd-dev.log`
- Swift app logs: `/tmp/irrlicht-app-dev.log`
